title: elaphoglossum barteri
intro: Elaphoglossum barteri, a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. Traditionally, Elaphoglossum barteri has been used in folk medicine, particularly in West Africa, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum barteri include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect the body from oxidative stress. Elaphoglossum barteri can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, where the leaves and stems are steeped in hot water to create a medicinal brew that can be consumed to harness its health benefits.
